title: C4: Lint #23 narrow_no_paths + #24 relevance_paths_dangling + #25 relevance_paths_drift
description: Three new doctor lint checks. #23 narrow_no_paths: narrow entries with relevance_paths: [] → warning only. #24 relevance_paths_dangling: expand each glob via fast-glob, count matches. Dangling = zero matches. --apply-lint removes the glob; if entry's relevance_paths becomes empty, auto-degrade scope narrow→broad. Emit knowledge_path_dangled event + (when triggered) knowledge_scope_degraded event. #25 relevance_paths_drift: git log --diff-filter=R --name-status --since='30 days ago' finds renames; cross-reference against narrow relevance_paths. Report only (rename heuristic too noisy for auto-mutation). Suggests fab_review.modify in report text. Anchor: doctor.ts:285-305 + handoff.json#acceptance_criteria for C4.

files.change: Add inspectNarrowNoPaths (#23), inspectRelevancePathsDangling (#24), inspectRelevancePathsDrift (#25); register in checks array at line 285-305
Add knowledge_path_dangled event with fields stable_id, removed_glob (reuse knowledge_scope_degraded from TASK-012)

implementation: Add event type knowledge_path_dangled to event-ledger.ts
Implement inspectNarrowNoPaths(): iterate canonical entries; collect those with relevance_scope='narrow' && relevance_paths.length === 0; warning only

Implement inspectRelevancePathsDangling(): for each narrow entry, expand each glob via fast-glob; mark dangling globs; in --apply-lint mode: rewrite entry frontmatter to remove dangling globs; if relevance_paths becomes empty → auto-degrade scope=broad; emit knowledge_path_dangled per removed glob; emit knowledge_scope_degraded if degrade happens

Implement inspectRelevancePathsDrift(): exec git log --diff-filter=R --name-status --since='30 days ago'; parse rename mappings; for each narrow entry, check if any relevance_paths matches a renamed source; report only (no mutation); include 'suggest: fab_review.modify <stable_id>' in message
Wire 3 new checks into the doctor.ts checks array (additive pattern at lines 285-305)
Add unit tests: #23 catches narrow-no-paths; #24 detects + auto-removes + auto-degrades; #25 reports rename suggestions
Document each new check in doctor's output legend

reference.examples: Existing lint #21 stale-pending shape; reuse fast-glob import for #24

criteria: doctor lint #23 narrow_no_paths: narrow entry with empty relevance_paths emits warning
doctor lint #24 relevance_paths_dangling: --apply-lint removes dangling globs and auto-degrades scope to broad when all paths danged; emits knowledge_path_dangled + knowledge_scope_degraded events
doctor lint #25 relevance_paths_drift: report-only, suggests fab_review.modify
knowledge_path_dangled event includes stable_id, removed_glob fields
Unit tests cover all three checks with both report and apply-lint modes (where applicable)

rationale.chosen_approach: Three additive inspect+check pairs; auto-mutation only for #24 (path deletion is fact); #25 stays report-only because rename heuristic is noisy
decision_factors: #23 is informational (narrow without paths may be intentional pre-Phase-1.5)
#25 git rename heuristic has false positives — auto-mutation unsafe
rationale.tradeoffs: #25 leaves manual cleanup to user; acceptable for rc.5 (LLM-driven monthly refresh deferred to follow-up)

manual_checks: Run doctor on test repo with mixed entry states; verify all 3 checks fire correctly

risks.description: fast-glob performance with many narrow entries × many globs
risks.mitigation: Batch glob expansion; cache results within single doctor run

risks.description: git log unavailable in non-git working copies
risks.mitigation: Try/catch git invocation; skip #25 with info message if no git
